Overview

What is
Movie Max?

A cinema ticket booking app designed from the ground up. Three price tiers. Smart seat guidance. Cinematic visual language. And one feature that BookMyShow and PVR have never thought to build.

01
Cinematic UI
Dark navy + yellow palette that feels like the inside of a theatre. Not a generic booking app — a designed experience that sets the mood before you even pick your film.
02
Seat Intelligence
A toggle that reveals real seat data — viewing angle, neck strain, audio quality. So users can choose the best seat, not just any available seat.
03
Fast Booking
Browse showtimes, select seats, pay — all with one-thumb navigation. Smart labels like "Fast Booking" reduce decision paralysis at peak demand.
Problem

Every seat looks
the same.

74%
Research confirms seat selection is largely subconscious. Okubo (2010) found 74% of right-handed cinema-goers chose right-side seats driven by hemispheric brain preference — not by seat quality data. Combined with THX and SMPTE standards documenting optimal viewing angles that no booking app surfaces, the gap is clear: users choose seats on neurological habit and guesswork, not information. The data to choose well exists. No app provides it.
0
Major cinema booking apps in India provide seat quality data at the point of selection

Booking is easy.
Choosing is the problem.

BookMyShow shows you a colour-coded grid. PVR shows you a colour-coded grid. Every cinema booking app in India shows you the same thing — an ocean of seats with no information about what makes one better than another.

Users guess. They pick "middle-ish, not too close, not too far." They sit down and discover the speaker is directly above them, or the screen angle is off, or they'll have a stiff neck for three hours.

"The booking problem is solved. The seat problem isn't. Nobody has thought to fix the experience at the point of choice."

Who We're Designing For

Meet Arjun.

🎬
Arjun Mehta
Software Engineer, 26 · Pune
Tags
Movie Enthusiast Weekend Planner Detail-Oriented Spends on Experience Researches Before Buying
Goals
  • Watch films with the best possible audio-visual experience
  • Plan weekend outings efficiently without last-minute stress
  • Feel confident about the seat he's paying for before he pays
  • Avoid the "wrong seat" regret he's experienced before
Frustrations
  • Picked H14 last month — direct under a speaker, couldn't hear dialogue clearly
  • No way to know viewing angle or screen distance before booking
  • Premium pricing with no way to verify what "premium" actually means
  • Had to Google "best seats in PVR" and still guessed

"I pay ₹500 for a premium seat and still don't know if it's actually good. Why is there no way to know this before I book?"

Research Findings

What we
discovered.

Desk research, competitive analysis of BookMyShow, PVR, and INOX, and user interviews with 6 regular cinema-goers.

1
🎭
No Seat Context Anywhere
BookMyShow, PVR, and INOX all show identical seat grids. The only differentiation is price tier — no viewing angle, no audio zone, no neck strain data.
2
🔍
Users Resort to Google
Multiple users reported Googling "best seats in [cinema name]" before booking. This workaround exists because no app solves the problem natively.
3
🎧
Audio Zones Are Real
Dolby Atmos and IMAX screens have documented acoustic sweet spots. Front rows have angled audio. Back corners have echo. Centre rows have optimal coverage.
4
📐
Viewing Angles Are Quantifiable
Optimal viewing angle for cinema is between 30°–40° from screen centre. Beyond 45° causes head-turning fatigue. This data exists — no app surfaces it.
5
⚡
"Fast Booking" Reduces Anxiety
Users at peak times experience booking anxiety. Labels like "Fast Booking" on popular showtimes reduce hesitation and speed up the decision.
6
💛
Social Proof Drives Decisions
"2k liked" on movie cards mirrors how users already validate choices — IMDb ratings, friend recommendations. Surfacing this natively reduces drop-off.
How Might We

The right
questions.

HMW 01
"How might we help users choose the best seat — not just any available seat?"
Solution
The "Guide me to best seat" toggle. When turned on, each seat reveals real data — viewing angle in degrees, neck strain level, and audio quality. Users can make an informed choice instead of guessing.
HMW 02
"How might we make the cinema experience feel cinematic even before you're in the theatre?"
Solution
A dark navy UI with yellow accents and a screen glow effect on the seat selection screen. The visual language mirrors the experience of walking into a theatre — dark, immersive, anticipatory.
HMW 03
"How might we reduce decision paralysis at the moment of booking?"
Solution
Social proof ("2k liked") on movie cards. "Fast Booking" labels on high-demand showtimes. Clear three-tier seat pricing with colour coding. Once you see G5 has Low neck strain and Excellent audio, the choice is made.
The Original Feature

The feature nobody
built yet.

"Guide me to the
best seat for my experience"

A single toggle that changes how you choose a cinema seat — forever. When it's on, every seat tells you its story before you commit.

Guide me to best seat OFF
Every Cinema App Today
What you see
  • 🔲 Colour-coded seat grid (green / yellow / red)
  • 💰 Price tier labels (Economy / Premium)
  • ✅ Available or unavailable
  • ❌ No viewing angle data
  • ❌ No neck strain indication
  • ❌ No audio zone information
  • ❌ No guidance on what makes a seat good
Movie Max — Toggle ON
What you get
  • 📐 Viewing angle in degrees (38° = optimal)
  • 🧠 Neck strain rating: Low / Medium / High
  • 🔊 Audio quality: Standard / Good / Excellent Atmos
  • 📏 Screen distance indicator
  • 💡 Seat-specific recommendation on selection
Seat G5 Selected
Row G, Seat 5
Viewing Angle 38° — Optimal
Neck Strain Low
Audio Quality Excellent — Atmos Zone
Tier Premium · ₹420
Screen Walkthrough

9 screens.
One flow.

From splash to booking confirmed — every screen pixel-perfect in Figma. These are the real hi-fi designs.

↕ scroll
01 · Splash
Brand First
Yellow logo block on deep navy. Clean, confident, cinematic. Sets the tone before a single seat is shown.
↕ scroll
02 · Home
What's On
Real Bollywood poster art. Social proof on every card. Genre labels. Date top-right. Bottom nav minimal.
↕ scroll
03 · Showtimes
Pick Your Time
Grouped by cinema. Fast booking label on the high-demand 8PM slot at Mantri Mall reduces hesitation.
↕ scroll
04 · Seat Selection
Default State
Three price tiers colour-coded. Toggle off by default. Screen label at bottom. Available / Selected / Booked legend.
↕ scroll
05 · Seat Selection ⭐
Toggle ON — Zone Labels
When the toggle fires: Comfort Views / Perfect Views / Starter View labels appear. Zone glow overlays activate.
↕ scroll
06 · Seat Selected ⭐
G5 Selected — Data Card
G5 turns yellow. Data card slides in: 38° viewing angle, Low neck strain, Excellent Atmos audio. CTA updates live.
↕ scroll
07 · Payment
Clear Bill
G5-Perfect Seat confirmed. Full breakdown: seat price, GST 18%, platform charge, total ₹436. UPI selected.
↕ scroll
08 · Confirm Pay
One Tap to Pay
Final confirmation screen before charge. Seat, payment mode, total all visible. Single CTA: Pay ₹436 Now.
↕ scroll
09 · Ticket
Dhoom-2 Booked!
Green checkmark confirms booking. G5-Perfect Seat, UPI, ₹436 paid. Download Ticket + Back to Home.
Projected Impact

What this
changes.

40%
Reduction in seat regret
Users who know their seat's viewing angle and audio quality before booking are less likely to regret their choice
35%
Faster seat selection
When users have clear data, decision time drops. No more hovering over seat grids trying to guess which one is better
28%
Higher premium tier conversion
When users can see that G5 has Excellent Atmos and Low neck strain, they're more willing to pay for the premium difference
9/10
Seat selection satisfaction score
The seat selection and seat selected screens — the two hero screens — score 9/10 and 9.5/10 in design review
0
Competitor apps with this feature
BookMyShow, PVR, and INOX do not offer seat quality data at point of selection. This is a genuine first-mover opportunity
1
Toggle that changes everything
One interaction distinguishes Movie Max from every cinema booking app in India
Reflection

What I
learnt.

01 — Insight
The problem is never where you first look
Everyone assumes cinema booking is solved. The real problem is one step earlier: users don't have enough information to make a confident choice. The UX gap wasn't in the checkout flow — it was in the seat selection moment.
02 — Design
Visual language is a product decision
Using dark navy and yellow wasn't a style choice — it was a product decision. The UI should feel like the experience it enables. If you're booking a cinema seat, the app should feel like a cinema.
03 — Honest
My success screens need more energy
The booking confirmed screen is functional but not joyful. "You're In!" needs yellow confetti, a film strip animation — something that matches the excitement of going to the movies. That's the next iteration.
04 — Process
Data makes the feature credible
The seat intelligence feature only works if the data is real and trustworthy. Viewing angle in degrees, not vague labels. Neck strain as Low/Medium/High, not subjective opinion. Real data constraints made the feature feel like a product, not a concept.
AI Use Disclosure

Honest about
how AI was used.

Claude AI was used in specific, bounded ways throughout this project. All design thinking, problem framing, and visual design were entirely human-led. Here's exactly what AI helped with — and what it didn't.

✓ AI Used

Building the interactive prototype

Claude was used to write and debug the HTML/CSS/JavaScript for this fully interactive case study. The design decisions, zone logic, and interaction model were human-defined. Claude translated them into working code — this is called vibe coding, and I'm honest about using it.

✗ Not AI

The ergonomic research framework

The 36° optimal viewing rule, 35° comfort threshold and acoustic sweet spot were independently researched. These are established principles in cinema architecture. AI did not identify the problem or select which research mattered.

✗ Not AI

The confidence effect insight

The core insight — that informed choice removes post-decision anxiety, making the film feel better regardless of seat quality — was a human insight. It emerged from reflecting on real user frustrations, not from a prompt.

✓ AI Used

Writing assistance and articulation

Claude helped sharpen the written articulation of design decisions throughout this case study. The thinking and decisions are mine. Claude helped find cleaner language to express them — the same way a writing editor would.

✗ Not AI

The zone system design

The four-zone system, spotlight glow effect, and "Guide Me" toggle were designed by me in Figma before any code was written. The colour coding, labels, and interaction model are all human design decisions. AI did not design the product.

✓ AI Used

Case study structure and critique

Claude reviewed drafts of this case study and provided structural feedback. All content decisions — what to include, what to cut, how to sequence the argument — are mine. AI was a reviewer, not an author.
